questions.question: What does a transpiler do?
options: Converts modern syntax into another version of JavaScript
questions.explanation: Transpilers rewrite code into output that is easier for target environments to run.

questions.question: Why is SWC popular in Next.js?
options: It is a fast compiler for modern frontend builds
questions.explanation: SWC is used because it offers very fast compilation for modern JavaScript and TypeScript workflows.

questions.question: What do source maps help with?
options: Debugging compiled code by pointing back to the original source
questions.explanation: Source maps map generated code back to the original files so debugging stays practical.

questions.question: What is the difference between transpiling and bundling?
options: Transpiling rewrites syntax; bundling combines files and assets
Bundling rewrites syntax; transpiling creates images
questions.explanation: Transpiling and bundling are related but separate steps in the build pipeline.
